presentation.overrideGuidance: Optional. When included, set up a communicative scenario or introduce useful expressions and gambits for the session topic. Keep brief — no more than 5 minutes.
presentation.notes: Presentation is optional in conversation lessons. The session may move directly from warm-up to practice if no new language needs to be introduced.

practice.overrideGuidance: Controlled oral practice using structured role-plays and guided dialogues. Start with closed role-play before moving to open scenarios.

production.overrideGuidance: Free communicative activity where the student speaks independently. Use open role-play, debate, or thematic free conversation.

levelVariations.A1: Use closed role-plays (EO-01) and guided dialogues only. Provide phrase banks. High tolerance for L1 use.
levelVariations.A2: Introduce open role-play (EO-02) with a clear scenario and vocabulary support.
levelVariations.B1: Move toward free conversation (EO-08) on familiar topics. Introduce negotiation and problem-solving (EO-07).
levelVariations.B2: Open discussion, debate (EO-06), and structured interviews (EO-03) are all appropriate.
levelVariations.C1: Debate, mediation (EO-09), and sustained monologue (EO-05). Minimal scaffolding.
levelVariations.C2: Peer-level discussion, mediation (EO-09), register-aware extended discourse. No scaffolding needed.

warmUp.overrideGuidance: Activate prior knowledge of the target grammar structure. Use an inductive noticing task or a quick diagnostic exercise.

presentation.overrideGuidance: Introduce the grammar rule through examples and guided discovery. Use noticing (GR-08) at B1+, paradigms (GR-05) at A1-A2. Explain meaning, form, and use.

practice.overrideGuidance: Controlled grammar practice. Order from mechanical (fill-in-blank, conjugation) to meaningful (sentence transformation, error correction). Use at least 3 different exercise formats.

production.overrideGuidance: Communicative task requiring natural use of the target structure. Guided dialogue or structured interview at lower levels; free conversation or role-play at higher levels.

levelVariations.A1: Focus on paradigms (GR-05) and basic fill-in-blank (GR-01). No error correction or transformation tasks.
levelVariations.A2: Add matching (GR-06) and simple word ordering (GR-07). Still no error correction.
levelVariations.B1: Add sentence transformation (GR-03) and error correction (GR-04). Introduce inductive discovery (GR-08).
levelVariations.B2: Nuance tasks: aspect, mood, register. Use GR-08 and GR-10 (paradigm from text).
levelVariations.C1: Focus on pragmatic grammar, discourse markers, and register. GR-08 and GR-10 are the primary discovery tools.
levelVariations.C2: Sociolinguistic nuance. Student analyses and categorises independently. GR-10 (complete paradigm from text).

warmUp.overrideGuidance: Pre-reading activation only. Activate background knowledge, predict content from the title, or brainstorm vocabulary around the topic. No grammar drills.

presentation.overrideGuidance: Embed a complete reading passage with CEFR-appropriate length in this section (A1: 100-150 words, A2: 150-250 words, B1+: 300-500 words). First read for gist, second read for detail. Pre-teach blocking vocabulary before reading.
presentation.notes: The reading passage TEXT MUST appear in this section. Do not move the passage to practice or production.

practice.overrideGuidance: Comprehension questions covering three types: (1) factual — explicitly stated in the text, (2) inferential — requiring reading between the lines, (3) vocabulary in context — meaning of a word or phrase as used in the passage. Include at least 2 questions of each type.

production.overrideGuidance: Free-production task connected to the passage topic. Short written response, opinion discussion, or creative extension. Student works independently.

wrapUp.overrideGuidance: Student summarises the passage in 1-2 sentences. Brief discussion of the author's purpose or the student's reaction.

levelVariations.A1: Very short passages (100-150 words). Factual questions only (CE-01, CE-02). Heavy vocabulary pre-teaching required.
levelVariations.A2: Short passages (150-250 words). Add true/false questions (CE-03). Simple vocabulary in context.
levelVariations.B1: 300-500 word authentic-style passages. Include inferential questions (CE-07). Vocabulary in context (VOC-04).
levelVariations.B2: Authentic texts. Inference (CE-07), register identification (CE-08), ordering paragraphs (CE-05).
levelVariations.C1: Complex authentic texts. Emphasise critical reading (CE-09), inference (CE-07), and register analysis (CE-08).
levelVariations.C2: Ungraded authentic texts. Critical reading (CE-09), register (CE-08), and mediation-based responses.

restrictions.reason: Ludic activities are inappropriate in reading comprehension lessons where the focus is on text processing and comprehension skills.

warmUp.overrideGuidance: Activate writing-related background knowledge. Discuss a sample text type, analyse a model, or brainstorm ideas for the writing task.

presentation.overrideGuidance: Present a model text of the target genre. Analyse its structure, register, and key phrases. Students identify organisational features and useful expressions.

practice.overrideGuidance: Guided writing tasks building toward the production text. Sentence completion, paragraph building, or structured exercises focusing on the target writing features.

production.overrideGuidance: The student produces a complete written text of the target type independently. Specify text type, length (word count), and communicative purpose clearly.
production.notes: Production is the central section of this template. This is where the student writes independently. Do not abbreviate or collapse this section.

wrapUp.overrideGuidance: Peer review or self-correction using the lesson success criteria. Student identifies one strength and one area to improve in their written text.

levelVariations.A1: Guided writing with full model (EE-01). Short functional texts (EE-03). Sentence completion tasks. L1 support appropriate.
levelVariations.A2: Semi-guided writing: notes, postcards, short messages (EE-03). Paragraph completion (EE-02).
levelVariations.B1: Guided letter/email (EE-06), short narration (EE-05), or simple opinion paragraph (EE-07).
levelVariations.B2: Letter/email (EE-06), argumentative paragraph (EE-07), or summary (EE-08). Register awareness introduced.
levelVariations.C1: Full autonomy: argumentative essay (EE-07), register transformation (EE-09), creative text (EE-10).
levelVariations.C2: Written mediation (EE-11), complex register transformation (EE-09), and near-native creative production (EE-10).

restrictions.reason: Oral exercises are excluded in this template to keep focus on written production outcomes.

warmUp.overrideGuidance: Review the exam format, the target task type, and the scoring criteria. Briefly discuss what the examiner is looking for. No casual icebreakers or conversation warm-ups.

presentation.overrideGuidance: Teach the strategy for the target exam task (e.g. essay structure, formal letter conventions, skimming for gist). Use authentic exam-task examples. Formal register throughout.

practice.overrideGuidance: Timed written practice under exam conditions. All tasks must be written (essay, formal letter, reading comprehension, gap-fill). Debrief answers with reference to the mark scheme after each task.
practice.notes: Timer is mandatory: specify an explicit time limit (e.g. '15 minutes') for every timed practice task in this section.

production.overrideGuidance: Full written exam task the student attempts independently. Specify a time limit (in minutes) and a target word count. Task type must match the exam format: opinion essay, formal letter, short report, or similar written genre. Do NOT use oral role-play or conversation activities.

wrapUp.overrideGuidance: Student self-assesses against the mark scheme criteria. Teacher identifies one strength and one area to improve before the next session.

levelVariations.B1: DELE B1 task types: short emails, opinion paragraphs, simple formal letters. Word counts 80-150 words.
levelVariations.B2: DELE B2 task types: formal letters, opinion essays, short reports. Word counts 150-250 words.
levelVariations.C1: DELE C1 tasks: argumentative essays, written mediation, formal reports. Higher word counts (250-350 words).
levelVariations.C2: DELE C2 tasks: near-native precision, complex register, mediation. Word counts 300-400 words.

restrictions.reason: Ludic activities are inappropriate in exam preparation lessons where the focus is on exam-format written tasks.

warmUp.overrideGuidance: Activate existing vocabulary on the theme. Brainstorm, mind-map, or categorize words the student already knows. Identify gaps before introducing new items.

presentation.overrideGuidance: Introduce new vocabulary items in context. Use semantic fields, collocations, and word families. Present words in sentences or short texts, never as isolated lists.

practice.overrideGuidance: Vocabulary practice exercises. Prefer contextual exercises (words in sentences, vocabulary in text) over decontextualized drills.

production.overrideGuidance: Communicative task requiring natural use of the target vocabulary. Oral description, thematic free conversation, or a short written text using the learned items.

wrapUp.overrideGuidance: Consolidation activity. Student recalls as many new words as possible (e.g. mind map from memory) and notes items for spaced review.

levelVariations.A1: Concrete, everyday vocabulary (food, family, home). L1 translations appropriate (VOC-01). Picture-word matching (VOC-02).
levelVariations.A2: Vocabulary in sentences. Synonyms/antonyms at basic level (VOC-06). Categorization (VOC-03).
levelVariations.B1: Collocations (VOC-07), word formation prefixes/suffixes (VOC-11), vocabulary in context (VOC-04).
levelVariations.B2: Advanced collocations, register-sensitive choices, fixed phrases (VOC-10).
levelVariations.C1: Idioms and fixed expressions (VOC-10), advanced word formation (VOC-11), near-native lexical range.
levelVariations.C2: Register-aware lexical choices, academic and professional vocabulary, idiomatic expression at native level.

warmUp.overrideGuidance: Cultural activation. Use a provocative image, headline, or question to stimulate interest in the cultural topic. Encourage the student to share their own cultural perspective.

presentation.overrideGuidance: Present cultural content through a text, video description, or curated facts. Include both factual information and an opportunity for the student to respond to the content.

practice.overrideGuidance: Cultural comprehension and analysis tasks. Compare cultural practices, analyse a cultural text, or explore a societal theme. Avoid purely mechanical grammar drills.

production.overrideGuidance: Intercultural reflection or discussion. Student compares the target culture with their own, expresses a reasoned opinion, or produces a short written reflection.

wrapUp.overrideGuidance: Student reflects on one thing they found surprising or interesting about the cultural topic. Brief discussion of how it connects to their own culture or experience.

levelVariations.A1: Simple cultural facts with visual support. Focus on familiar everyday culture (food, celebrations, greetings). Very short texts.
levelVariations.A2: Short authentic-style cultural texts. Basic intercultural comparison (PRAG-04). Everyday societal topics.
levelVariations.B1: Authentic or near-authentic cultural texts. Intercultural comparison and opinion expression (PRAG-04).
levelVariations.B2: Complex societal topics (inequality, environment, traditions). Inference (CE-07) and critical discussion (EO-06).
levelVariations.C1: Nuanced cultural and literary topics. Oral mediation (EO-09) and critical analysis (CE-09).
levelVariations.C2: Identity, politics, literature, and philosophy. Mediation (EO-09), register-aware argumentation, near-native precision.
